68	# Returns: JSON with period start and current time
69	get_daily_period() {
70	    local next_reset="${1:?Missing next_reset timestamp}"
71	    local current_ts=$(date +%s)
72	
73	    # Extract the time-of-day from the reset timestamp
74	    # Strategy: Get the hour offset from midnight in the reset's timezone
75	
76	    # First, get the weekly period to understand the reset schedule
77	    local weekly_period=$(get_anthropic_period "$next_reset")
78	    local weekly_start=$(echo "$weekly_period" | jq -r '.start')
79	
80	    # Calculate seconds since start of week (day-of-week offset)
81	    local week_offset=$(( (current_ts - weekly_start) % WEEK_DURATION_SECONDS ))
82	
83	    # Calculate today's reset time by finding the last occurrence of the daily reset hour
84	    # Start from the weekly reset time and add days until we're close to now
85	    local daily_reset=$weekly_start
86	
87	    # Fast forward to approximately today (within this week)
88	    while [[ $((daily_reset + DAY_DURATION_SECONDS)) -le $current_ts ]]; do
89	        daily_reset=$((daily_reset + DAY_DURATION_SECONDS))
90	    done
91	
92	    # If we're before today's reset time, use yesterday's reset
93	    if [[ $current_ts -lt $daily_reset ]]; then
94	        daily_reset=$((daily_reset - DAY_DURATION_SECONDS))
95	    fi
96	
97	    printf '{"start":%d,"end":%d}\n' "$daily_reset" "$current_ts"
98	}
99	
100	# Calculate daily cost using ccusage blocks
101	# Filters blocks from today's baseline reset time to current time
102	# Usage: get_daily_cost <next_reset_timestamp> [cache_duration_seconds]
103	# Returns: Total cost for current daily period
104	get_daily_cost() {
105	    local next_reset="${1:?Missing next_reset timestamp}"
106	    local cache_duration="${2:-300}"  # Default 5 minutes, configurable
107	
108	    # Get script directory (when sourced, use caller's directory)
109	    local utils_dir="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
110	    local cache_file="$utils_dir/../data/.daily_cache"
111	    local cache_duration_minutes=$((cache_duration / 60))
112	
113	    # Get current daily period boundaries
114	    local period_data=$(get_daily_period "$next_reset")
115	    local period_start=$(echo "$period_data" | jq -r '.start')
116	    local period_end=$(echo "$period_data" | jq -r '.end')
117	
118	    # Check if cache exists and is fresh
119	    if [[ -f "$cache_file" ]]; then
120	        local cache_age=$(find "$cache_file" -mmin -${cache_duration_minutes} 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
121	        if [[ $cache_age -gt 0 ]]; then
122	            # Read cache and validate format: timestamp|period_start|period_end|daily_cost
123	            local cache_content=$(cat "$cache_file")
124	            local cached_period_start=$(echo "$cache_content" | cut -d'|' -f2)
125	            local cached_cost=$(echo "$cache_content" | cut -d'|' -f4)
126	
127	            # Validate: same period AND valid number
128	            if [[ "$cached_period_start" == "$period_start" ]] && [[ "$cached_cost" =~ ^[0-9]+\.?[0-9]*$ ]]; then
129	                echo "$cached_cost"
130	                return 0
131	            fi
132	            # Cache invalid (wrong period or corrupted) - fall through to recalculate
133	        fi
134	    fi
135	
136	    # Convert period start to ISO 8601 format for comparison with ccusage
137	    local start_iso=$(timestamp_to_iso "$period_start")
138	
139	    # Get all blocks from ccusage
140	    local blocks_data=$(cd ~ && npx --yes ccusage blocks --json --offline 2>/dev/null | awk '/^{/,0')
141	
142	    if [[ -z "$blocks_data" || "$blocks_data" == "null" ]]; then
143	        echo "0.00"
144	        return
145	    fi
146	
147	    # Filter blocks where startTime >= period_start OR blocks that overlap the period
148	    # For overlapping blocks, include the full cost (conservative estimate)
149	    local period_end_iso=$(timestamp_to_iso "$period_end")
150	    local daily_cost=$(echo "$blocks_data" | jq -r --arg start_iso "$start_iso" --arg end_iso "$period_end_iso" '
151	        [.blocks[] |
152	         select(
153	           # Block starts within daily period
154	           (.startTime >= $start_iso) or
155	           # OR block is active and overlaps with daily period (endTime > period_start)
156	           (.endTime > $start_iso and .startTime < $end_iso)
157	         ) |
158	         .costUSD
159	        ] | add // 0
160	    ')
161	
162	    # Format to 2 decimal places
163	    daily_cost=$(printf "%.2f" "$daily_cost")
164	
165	    # Atomic cache write with period metadata
166	    # Format: timestamp|period_start|period_end|daily_cost
167	    local current_ts=$(date +%s)
168	    echo "${current_ts}|${period_start}|${period_end}|${daily_cost}" > "${cache_file}.tmp"
169	    mv "${cache_file}.tmp" "$cache_file"
170	
171	    echo "$daily_cost"
172	}
173	
174	# Calculate official Anthropic weekly cost (matches console reset schedule)
175	# Uses ccusage blocks data filtered by official reset period
176	# Usage: get_official_weekly_cost <next_reset_timestamp> [cache_duration_seconds]
177	# Returns: Total cost for current Anthropic weekly period
178	get_official_weekly_cost() {
179	    local next_reset="${1:?Missing next_reset timestamp}"
180	    local cache_duration="${2:-300}"  # Default 5 minutes, configurable
181	
182	    # Get script directory (when sourced, use caller's directory)
183	    local utils_dir="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
184	    local cache_file="$utils_dir/../data/.official_weekly_cache"
185	    local cache_duration_minutes=$((cache_duration / 60))
186	
187	    # Get current Anthropic period boundaries
188	    local period_data=$(get_anthropic_period "$next_reset")
189	    local period_start=$(echo "$period_data" | jq -r '.start')
190	    local period_end=$(echo "$period_data" | jq -r '.end')
191	
192	    # Check if cache exists and is fresh
193	    if [[ -f "$cache_file" ]]; then
194	        # Fix: Use -mmin (minutes) instead of -mtime (days)
195	        local cache_age=$(find "$cache_file" -mmin -${cache_duration_minutes} 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
196	        if [[ $cache_age -gt 0 ]]; then
197	            # Read cache and validate format: timestamp|period_start|period_end|weekly_cost
198	            local cache_content=$(cat "$cache_file")
199	            local cached_period_start=$(echo "$cache_content" | cut -d'|' -f2)
200	            local cached_cost=$(echo "$cache_content" | cut -d'|' -f4)
201	
202	            # Validate: same period AND valid number
203	            if [[ "$cached_period_start" == "$period_start" ]] && [[ "$cached_cost" =~ ^[0-9]+\.?[0-9]*$ ]]; then
204	                echo "$cached_cost"
205	                return 0
206	            fi
207	            # Cache invalid (wrong period or corrupted) - fall through to recalculate
208	        fi
209	    fi
210	
211	    # Convert period start to ISO 8601 format for comparison with ccusage
212	    local start_iso=$(timestamp_to_iso "$period_start")
213	
214	    # Get all blocks from ccusage
215	    local blocks_data=$(cd ~ && npx --yes ccusage blocks --json --offline 2>/dev/null | awk '/^{/,0')
216	
217	    if [[ -z "$blocks_data" || "$blocks_data" == "null" ]]; then
218	        echo "0.00"
219	        return
220	    fi
221	
222	    # Filter blocks where startTime >= period_start and sum costs
223	    local weekly_cost=$(echo "$blocks_data" | jq -r --arg start_iso "$start_iso" '
224	        [.blocks[] |
225	         select(.startTime >= $start_iso) |
226	         .costUSD
227	        ] | add // 0
228	    ')
229	
230	    # Format to 2 decimal places
231	    weekly_cost=$(printf "%.2f" "$weekly_cost")
232	
233	    # Atomic cache write with period metadata
234	    # Format: timestamp|period_start|period_end|weekly_cost
235	    local current_ts=$(date +%s)
236	    echo "${current_ts}|${period_start}|${period_end}|${weekly_cost}" > "${cache_file}.tmp"
237	    mv "${cache_file}.tmp" "$cache_file"
238	
239	    echo "$weekly_cost"
240	}
241	
242	# Calculate weekly recommended usage based on cycle start
243	# Returns static value for current cycle: weekly_avail_at_cycle_start / ceil(cycles_left_from_cycle_start)
244	# Usage: get_weekly_recommend <next_reset_timestamp> <weekly_limit> <weekly_baseline_pct> [cache_duration_seconds]
245	# Returns: Recommended daily usage percentage
246	get_weekly_recommend() {
247	    local next_reset="${1:?Missing next_reset timestamp}"
248	    local weekly_limit="${2:?Missing weekly_limit}"
249	    local weekly_baseline_pct="${3:-0}"
250	    local cache_duration="${4:-300}"
251	
252	    # Cache file per cycle
253	    local cache_file="${CACHE_DIR:-.}/.weekly_recommend_cache"
254	
255	    # Get current cycle period
256	    local period_data=$(get_daily_period "$next_reset")
257	    local cycle_start=$(echo "$period_data" | jq -r '.start')
258	
259	    # Check cache validity
260	    if [[ -f "$cache_file" ]]; then
261	        IFS='|' read -r cached_ts cached_cycle_start cached_value < "$cache_file"
262	        local current_ts=$(date +%s)
263	
264	        # Cache valid if: same cycle AND within cache duration
265	        if [[ "$cached_cycle_start" == "$cycle_start" ]] && \
266	           [[ $((current_ts - cached_ts)) -lt $cache_duration ]] && \
267	           [[ "$cached_value" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
268	            echo "$cached_value"
269	            return 0
270	        fi
271	    fi
272	
273	    # Get weekly cost at cycle start by filtering blocks
274	    local cycle_start_iso=$(timestamp_to_iso "$cycle_start")
275	    local weekly_period=$(get_anthropic_period "$next_reset")
276	    local period_start=$(echo "$weekly_period" | jq -r '.start')
277	    local period_start_iso=$(timestamp_to_iso "$period_start")
278	
279	    # Get blocks and filter up to cycle start
280	    local blocks_data=$(cd ~ && npx --yes ccusage blocks --json --offline 2>/dev/null | awk '/^{/,0')
281	
282	    if [[ -z "$blocks_data" || "$blocks_data" == "null" ]]; then
283	        echo "0"
284	        return
285	    fi
286	
287	    # Calculate weekly cost at cycle start (from blocks before cycle started)
288	    local weekly_cost_at_cycle_start=$(echo "$blocks_data" | jq -r --arg start_iso "$period_start_iso" --arg end_iso "$cycle_start_iso" '
289	        [.blocks[] |
290	         select(
291	           (.startTime >= $start_iso) and
292	           (.startTime < $end_iso)
293	         ) |
294	         .costUSD
295	        ] | add // 0
296	    ')
297	
298	    # Apply baseline offset (same as current week calculation)
299	    if [ "$(awk "BEGIN {print ($weekly_baseline_pct != 0)}")" = "1" ]; then
300	        local baseline_cost=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", ($weekly_limit * $weekly_baseline_pct) / 100}")
301	        weekly_cost_at_cycle_start=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", $weekly_cost_at_cycle_start + $baseline_cost}")
302	    fi
303	
304	    # Calculate available budget at cycle start
305	    local avail_cost_at_start=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", $weekly_limit - $weekly_cost_at_cycle_start}")
306	    local avail_pct_at_start=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", ($avail_cost_at_start / $weekly_limit) * 100}")
307	
308	    # Calculate cycles left from cycle start to reset
309	    local time_from_cycle_start_to_reset=$((next_reset - cycle_start))
310	    local cycles_left=$(awk "BEGIN {hours = $time_from_cycle_start_to_reset / 3600; cycles = hours / 24; print (cycles == int(cycles)) ? int(cycles) : int(cycles) + 1}")
311	
312	    # Calculate recommend value
313	    local recommend_value=0
314	    if [ "$cycles_left" -gt 0 ]; then
315	        recommend_value=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.0f\", $avail_pct_at_start / $cycles_left}")
316	    fi
317	
318	    # Cache the result
319	    local current_ts=$(date +%s)
320	    echo "${current_ts}|${cycle_start}|${recommend_value}" > "${cache_file}.tmp"
321	    mv "${cache_file}.tmp" "$cache_file"
322	
323	    echo "$recommend_value"
324	}
325	
326	# Calculate monthly period boundaries
327	# Returns current monthly period from payment_cycle_start_date to next month
328	# Usage: get_monthly_period <payment_cycle_start_timestamp>
329	# Returns: JSON with {start, end} timestamps
330	get_monthly_period() {
331	    local cycle_start="${1:?Missing payment_cycle_start timestamp}"
332	
333	    # Get current time
334	    local current_time=$(date +%s)
335	
336	    # Extract date components from cycle start
337	    local cycle_start_iso=$(timestamp_to_iso "$cycle_start")
338	
339	    # Try GNU date first, then macOS date
340	    # Normalise ISO string for macOS date -j: Z → +0000, strip colon in +HH:MM
341	    local cycle_start_iso_norm=$(echo "$cycle_start_iso" | sed 's/Z$/+0000/; s/\([+-][0-9][0-9]\):\([0-9][0-9]\)$/\1\2/')
342	
343	    local cycle_day=$(date -d "$cycle_start_iso" "+%d" 2>/dev/null) || true
344	    if [ -z "$cycle_day" ]; then
345	        cycle_day=$(date -j -f "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z" "$cycle_start_iso_norm" "+%d" 2>/dev/null) || true
346	    fi
347	    if [ -z "$cycle_day" ]; then
348	        cycle_day=$(date -j -u -f "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S" "${cycle_start_iso%%[Z+]*}" "+%d" 2>/dev/null)
349	    fi
350	
351	    local cycle_time=$(date -d "$cycle_start_iso" "+%H:%M:%S" 2>/dev/null) || true
352	    if [ -z "$cycle_time" ]; then
353	        cycle_time=$(date -j -f "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z" "$cycle_start_iso_norm" "+%H:%M:%S" 2>/dev/null) || true
354	    fi
355	    if [ -z "$cycle_time" ]; then
356	        cycle_time=$(date -j -u -f "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S" "${cycle_start_iso%%[Z+]*}" "+%H:%M:%S" 2>/dev/null)
357	    fi
358	
359	    # Get current year and month
360	    local current_year=$(date -d "@$current_time" "+%Y" 2>/dev/null) || true
361	    if [ -z "$current_year" ]; then
362	        current_year=$(date -j -r "$current_time" "+%Y")
363	    fi
364	
365	    local current_month=$(date -d "@$current_time" "+%m" 2>/dev/null) || true
366	    if [ -z "$current_month" ]; then
367	        current_month=$(date -j -r "$current_time" "+%m")
368	    fi
369	
370	    # Build current month's cycle start (same day and time, current month)
371	    local this_month_cycle=$(date -d "${current_year}-${current_month}-${cycle_day} ${cycle_time}" "+%s" 2>/dev/null) || true
372	    if [ -z "$this_month_cycle" ]; then
373	        this_month_cycle=$(date -j -f "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S" "${current_year}-${current_month}-${cycle_day} ${cycle_time}" "+%s" 2>/dev/null)
374	    fi
375	
376	    # If current time is before this month's cycle start, use previous month
377	    if [ "$current_time" -lt "$this_month_cycle" ]; then
378	        # Go back one month
379	        if [ "$current_month" = "01" ]; then
380	            current_year=$((current_year - 1))
381	            current_month="12"
382	        else
383	            current_month=$(printf "%02d" $((10#$current_month - 1)))
384	        fi
385	        this_month_cycle=$(date -d "${current_year}-${current_month}-${cycle_day} ${cycle_time}" "+%s" 2>/dev/null) || true
386	        if [ -z "$this_month_cycle" ]; then
387	            this_month_cycle=$(date -j -f "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S" "${current_year}-${current_month}-${cycle_day} ${cycle_time}" "+%s" 2>/dev/null)
388	        fi
389	    fi
390	
391	    # Calculate next month's cycle start
392	    local next_year="$current_year"
393	    local next_month=$(printf "%02d" $((10#$current_month + 1)))
394	    if [ "$next_month" = "13" ]; then
395	        next_year=$((current_year + 1))
396	        next_month="01"
397	    fi
398	
399	    local next_month_cycle=$(date -d "${next_year}-${next_month}-${cycle_day} ${cycle_time}" "+%s" 2>/dev/null) || true
400	    if [ -z "$next_month_cycle" ]; then
401	        next_month_cycle=$(date -j -f "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S" "${next_year}-${next_month}-${cycle_day} ${cycle_time}" "+%s" 2>/dev/null)
402	    fi
403	
404	    # Return period boundaries
405	    echo "{\"start\": $this_month_cycle, \"end\": $next_month_cycle}"
406	}
407	
408	# Calculate monthly cost using ccusage blocks
409	# Filters blocks from payment cycle start to current time
410	# Usage: get_monthly_cost <payment_cycle_start_timestamp> [cache_duration_seconds]
411	# Returns: Total cost for current monthly period
412	get_monthly_cost() {
413	    local cycle_start="${1:?Missing payment_cycle_start timestamp}"
414	    local cache_duration="${2:-300}"  # Default 5 minutes
415	
416	    # Get script directory
417	    local utils_dir="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
418	    local cache_file="$utils_dir/../data/.monthly_cache"
419	    local cache_duration_minutes=$((cache_duration / 60))
420	
421	    # Get current monthly period boundaries
422	    local period_data=$(get_monthly_period "$cycle_start")
423	    local period_start=$(echo "$period_data" | jq -r '.start')
424	    local period_end=$(echo "$period_data" | jq -r '.end')
425	
426	    # Check if cache exists and is fresh
427	    if [[ -f "$cache_file" ]]; then
428	        local cache_age=$(find "$cache_file" -mmin -${cache_duration_minutes} 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
429	        if [[ $cache_age -gt 0 ]]; then
430	            # Read cache and validate format: timestamp|period_start|period_end|monthly_cost
431	            local cache_content=$(cat "$cache_file")
432	            local cached_period_start=$(echo "$cache_content" | cut -d'|' -f2)
433	            local cached_cost=$(echo "$cache_content" | cut -d'|' -f4)
434	
435	            # Validate: same period AND valid number
436	            if [[ "$cached_period_start" == "$period_start" ]] && [[ "$cached_cost" =~ ^[0-9]+\.?[0-9]*$ ]]; then
437	                echo "$cached_cost"
438	                return 0
439	            fi
440	        fi
441	    fi
442	
443	    # Cache miss - calculate from ccusage
444	    # Convert timestamps to ISO format for ccusage
445	    local start_iso=$(timestamp_to_iso "$period_start")
446	    local end_iso=$(timestamp_to_iso "$period_end")
447	
448	    # Get all blocks and filter by monthly period
449	    local blocks_data=$(cd ~ && npx --yes "ccusage@17.1.0" blocks --json --offline 2>/dev/null | awk '/^{/,0')
450	
451	    # Filter blocks within period and sum costs
452	    local monthly_cost=$(echo "$blocks_data" | jq -r --arg start "$start_iso" --arg end "$end_iso" '
453	        [.blocks[]? |
454	         select(.startTime >= $start and .startTime < $end) |
455	         .costUSD // 0
456	        ] | add // 0
457	    ')
458	
459	    # Format to 2 decimal places
460	    monthly_cost=$(printf "%.2f" "$monthly_cost")
461	
462	    # Atomic cache write with period metadata
463	    local current_ts=$(date +%s)
464	    echo "${current_ts}|${period_start}|${period_end}|${monthly_cost}" > "${cache_file}.tmp"
465	    mv "${cache_file}.tmp" "$cache_file"
466	
467	    echo "$monthly_cost"
468	}
469	
470	# Calculate session cost using ccusage blocks
471	# Derives session start from the first entry in the transcript file, then sums
472	# block costs from that time onwards.
473	# Usage: get_session_cost <transcript_path>
474	# Returns: Total cost for the current session (float, 2 decimal places)
475	get_session_cost() {
476	    local transcript_path="${1:-}"
477	
478	    # Derive session start from the transcript's first line timestamp
479	    local session_start_iso=""
480	    if [[ -n "$transcript_path" && -f "$transcript_path" ]]; then
481	        # Each transcript line is a JSON object; grab the first "timestamp" field
482	        session_start_iso=$(head -1 "$transcript_path" 2>/dev/null | \
483	            grep -o '"timestamp":"[^"]*"' | head -1 | \
484	            sed 's/"timestamp":"//; s/"//')
485	    fi
486	
487	    if [[ -z "$session_start_iso" ]]; then
488	        # No transcript or no timestamp found — report 0 rather than crashing
489	        echo "0.00"
490	        return 0
491	    fi
492	
493	    # Fetch all blocks (offline / cached pricing) and filter by session start
494	    local blocks_data
495	    blocks_data=$(cd ~ && npx --yes ccusage blocks --json --offline 2>/dev/null | awk '/^{/,0')
496	
497	    if [[ -z "$blocks_data" || "$blocks_data" == "null" ]]; then
498	        echo "0.00"
499	        return 0
500	    fi
501	
502	    # Sum costUSD for every block whose startTime >= session start
503	    local session_cost
504	    session_cost=$(echo "$blocks_data" | jq -r --arg start "$session_start_iso" '
505	        [.blocks[] |
506	         select(.startTime >= $start) |
507	         .costUSD
508	        ] | add // 0
509	    ')
510	
511	    printf "%.2f" "$session_cost"
512	}
513	
514	# Export functions for use in other scripts
515	if [[ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" != "${0}" ]]; then
516	    export -f timestamp_to_iso
517	    export -f iso_to_timestamp
518	    export -f get_anthropic_period
519	    export -f get_daily_period
520	    export -f get_monthly_period
521	    export -f get_daily_cost
522	    export -f get_official_weekly_cost
523	    export -f get_monthly_cost
524	    export -f get_weekly_recommend
525	    export -f get_session_cost
526	fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# statusline-utils.sh - Utility functions for official Anthropic weekly reset tracking
# This file provides ccusage_r scheme support for matching official console reset schedule

# Constants
readonly WEEK_DURATION_SECONDS=604800  # 7 days
readonly DAY_DURATION_SECONDS=86400    # 24 hours

# Convert Unix timestamp to ISO 8601 format
# Usage: timestamp_to_iso <timestamp>
# Returns: ISO 8601 string like "2025-10-01T15:00:00Z"
timestamp_to_iso() {
    local timestamp="${1:?Missing timestamp}"
    date -u -r "$timestamp" +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ" 2>/dev/null || \
        date -u -d "@$timestamp" +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ" 2>/dev/null || true
}

# Convert ISO 8601 timestamp to Unix timestamp
# Usage: iso_to_timestamp <iso_string>
# Returns: Unix timestamp (seconds since epoch)
iso_to_timestamp() {
    local iso_string="${1:?Missing ISO timestamp}"
    # Normalise timezone colon (+00:00 → +0000) for macOS %z compatibility
    local iso_normalised=$(echo "$iso_string" | sed 's/\([+-][0-9][0-9]\):\([0-9][0-9]\)$/\1\2/')
    # macOS (BSD date) - using -j prevents setting system time, -f specifies input format
    date -j -f "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z" "$iso_normalised" "+%s" 2>/dev/null || \
        date -j -u -f "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S" "${iso_string%%[+-Z]*}" "+%s" 2>/dev/null || \
        # GNU date - simpler syntax
        date -d "$iso_string" "+%s" 2>/dev/null || true
}

# Calculate current Anthropic weekly period boundaries
# Anthropic uses fixed reset cycles (e.g., Wed 3pm → Wed 3pm in America/Vancouver timezone)
# Usage: get_anthropic_period <next_reset_timestamp>
# Returns: JSON with period start, end, and progress percentage
get_anthropic_period() {
    local next_reset="${1:?Missing next_reset timestamp}"
    local current_ts=$(date +%s)

    # If we're past the reset time, calculate the next period
    if [[ $current_ts -ge $next_reset ]]; then
        # Calculate how many weeks past the reset we are
        local weeks_past=$(( (current_ts - next_reset) / WEEK_DURATION_SECONDS ))
        next_reset=$(( next_reset + (weeks_past + 1) * WEEK_DURATION_SECONDS ))
    # If we're before the reset, we might need to go back to find the current period
    elif [[ $current_ts -lt $(( next_reset - WEEK_DURATION_SECONDS )) ]]; then
        # Go back to find the right period
        while [[ $current_ts -lt $(( next_reset - WEEK_DURATION_SECONDS )) ]]; do
            next_reset=$(( next_reset - WEEK_DURATION_SECONDS ))
        done
    fi

    # Current period: [reset - 7 days, reset)
    local period_start=$(( next_reset - WEEK_DURATION_SECONDS ))
    local period_end=$next_reset

    # Calculate progress
    local elapsed=$(( current_ts - period_start ))
    local percent=$(( elapsed * 100 / WEEK_DURATION_SECONDS ))

    printf '{"start":%d,"end":%d,"percent":%d}\n' \
        "$period_start" "$period_end" "$percent"
}

# Calculate current daily period based on reset time
# Daily periods reset at the same time each day (e.g., 3pm daily for a 3pm weekly reset)
# Usage: get_daily_period <next_reset_timestamp>
# Returns: JSON with period start and current time
get_daily_period() {
    local next_reset="${1:?Missing next_reset timestamp}"
    local current_ts=$(date +%s)

    # Extract the time-of-day from the reset timestamp
    # Strategy: Get the hour offset from midnight in the reset's timezone

    # First, get the weekly period to understand the reset schedule
    local weekly_period=$(get_anthropic_period "$next_reset")
    local weekly_start=$(echo "$weekly_period" | jq -r '.start')

    # Calculate seconds since start of week (day-of-week offset)
    local week_offset=$(( (current_ts - weekly_start) % WEEK_DURATION_SECONDS ))

    # Calculate today's reset time by finding the last occurrence of the daily reset hour
    # Start from the weekly reset time and add days until we're close to now
    local daily_reset=$weekly_start

    # Fast forward to approximately today (within this week)
    while [[ $((daily_reset + DAY_DURATION_SECONDS)) -le $current_ts ]]; do
        daily_reset=$((daily_reset + DAY_DURATION_SECONDS))
    done

    # If we're before today's reset time, use yesterday's reset
    if [[ $current_ts -lt $daily_reset ]]; then
        daily_reset=$((daily_reset - DAY_DURATION_SECONDS))
    fi

    printf '{"start":%d,"end":%d}\n' "$daily_reset" "$current_ts"
}

# Calculate daily cost using ccusage blocks
# Filters blocks from today's baseline reset time to current time
# Usage: get_daily_cost <next_reset_timestamp> [cache_duration_seconds]
# Returns: Total cost for current daily period
get_daily_cost() {
    local next_reset="${1:?Missing next_reset timestamp}"
    local cache_duration="${2:-300}"  # Default 5 minutes, configurable

    # Get script directory (when sourced, use caller's directory)
    local utils_dir="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
    local cache_file="$utils_dir/../data/.daily_cache"
    local cache_duration_minutes=$((cache_duration / 60))

    # Get current daily period boundaries
    local period_data=$(get_daily_period "$next_reset")
    local period_start=$(echo "$period_data" | jq -r '.start')
    local period_end=$(echo "$period_data" | jq -r '.end')

    # Check if cache exists and is fresh
    if [[ -f "$cache_file" ]]; then
        local cache_age=$(find "$cache_file" -mmin -${cache_duration_minutes} 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
        if [[ $cache_age -gt 0 ]]; then
            # Read cache and validate format: timestamp|period_start|period_end|daily_cost
            local cache_content=$(cat "$cache_file")
            local cached_period_start=$(echo "$cache_content" | cut -d'|' -f2)
            local cached_cost=$(echo "$cache_content" | cut -d'|' -f4)

            # Validate: same period AND valid number
            if [[ "$cached_period_start" == "$period_start" ]] && [[ "$cached_cost" =~ ^[0-9]+\.?[0-9]*$ ]]; then
                echo "$cached_cost"
                return 0
            fi
            # Cache invalid (wrong period or corrupted) - fall through to recalculate
        fi
    fi

    # Convert period start to ISO 8601 format for comparison with ccusage
    local start_iso=$(timestamp_to_iso "$period_start")

    # Get all blocks from ccusage
    local blocks_data=$(cd ~ && npx --yes ccusage blocks --json --offline 2>/dev/null | awk '/^{/,0')

    if [[ -z "$blocks_data" || "$blocks_data" == "null" ]]; then
        echo "0.00"
        return
    fi

    # Filter blocks where startTime >= period_start OR blocks that overlap the period
    # For overlapping blocks, include the full cost (conservative estimate)
    local period_end_iso=$(timestamp_to_iso "$period_end")
    local daily_cost=$(echo "$blocks_data" | jq -r --arg start_iso "$start_iso" --arg end_iso "$period_end_iso" '
        [.blocks[] |
         select(
           # Block starts within daily period
           (.startTime >= $start_iso) or
           # OR block is active and overlaps with daily period (endTime > period_start)
           (.endTime > $start_iso and .startTime < $end_iso)
         ) |
         .costUSD
        ] | add // 0
    ')

    # Format to 2 decimal places
    daily_cost=$(printf "%.2f" "$daily_cost")

    # Atomic cache write with period metadata
    # Format: timestamp|period_start|period_end|daily_cost
    local current_ts=$(date +%s)
    echo "${current_ts}|${period_start}|${period_end}|${daily_cost}" > "${cache_file}.tmp"
    mv "${cache_file}.tmp" "$cache_file"

    echo "$daily_cost"
}

# Calculate official Anthropic weekly cost (matches console reset schedule)
# Uses ccusage blocks data filtered by official reset period
# Usage: get_official_weekly_cost <next_reset_timestamp> [cache_duration_seconds]
# Returns: Total cost for current Anthropic weekly period
get_official_weekly_cost() {
    local next_reset="${1:?Missing next_reset timestamp}"
    local cache_duration="${2:-300}"  # Default 5 minutes, configurable

    # Get script directory (when sourced, use caller's directory)
    local utils_dir="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
    local cache_file="$utils_dir/../data/.official_weekly_cache"
    local cache_duration_minutes=$((cache_duration / 60))

    # Get current Anthropic period boundaries
    local period_data=$(get_anthropic_period "$next_reset")
    local period_start=$(echo "$period_data" | jq -r '.start')
    local period_end=$(echo "$period_data" | jq -r '.end')

    # Check if cache exists and is fresh
    if [[ -f "$cache_file" ]]; then
        # Fix: Use -mmin (minutes) instead of -mtime (days)
        local cache_age=$(find "$cache_file" -mmin -${cache_duration_minutes} 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
        if [[ $cache_age -gt 0 ]]; then
            # Read cache and validate format: timestamp|period_start|period_end|weekly_cost
            local cache_content=$(cat "$cache_file")
            local cached_period_start=$(echo "$cache_content" | cut -d'|' -f2)
            local cached_cost=$(echo "$cache_content" | cut -d'|' -f4)

            # Validate: same period AND valid number
            if [[ "$cached_period_start" == "$period_start" ]] && [[ "$cached_cost" =~ ^[0-9]+\.?[0-9]*$ ]]; then
                echo "$cached_cost"
                return 0
            fi
            # Cache invalid (wrong period or corrupted) - fall through to recalculate
        fi
    fi

    # Convert period start to ISO 8601 format for comparison with ccusage
    local start_iso=$(timestamp_to_iso "$period_start")

    # Get all blocks from ccusage
    local blocks_data=$(cd ~ && npx --yes ccusage blocks --json --offline 2>/dev/null | awk '/^{/,0')

    if [[ -z "$blocks_data" || "$blocks_data" == "null" ]]; then
        echo "0.00"
        return
    fi

    # Filter blocks where startTime >= period_start and sum costs
    local weekly_cost=$(echo "$blocks_data" | jq -r --arg start_iso "$start_iso" '
        [.blocks[] |
         select(.startTime >= $start_iso) |
         .costUSD
        ] | add // 0
    ')

    # Format to 2 decimal places
    weekly_cost=$(printf "%.2f" "$weekly_cost")

    # Atomic cache write with period metadata
    # Format: timestamp|period_start|period_end|weekly_cost
    local current_ts=$(date +%s)
    echo "${current_ts}|${period_start}|${period_end}|${weekly_cost}" > "${cache_file}.tmp"
    mv "${cache_file}.tmp" "$cache_file"

# Calculate weekly recommended usage based on cycle start
# Returns static value for current cycle: weekly_avail_at_cycle_start / ceil(cycles_left_from_cycle_start)
# Usage: get_weekly_recommend <next_reset_timestamp> <weekly_limit> <weekly_baseline_pct> [cache_duration_seconds]
# Returns: Recommended daily usage percentage
get_weekly_recommend() {
    local next_reset="${1:?Missing next_reset timestamp}"
    local weekly_limit="${2:?Missing weekly_limit}"
    local weekly_baseline_pct="${3:-0}"
    local cache_duration="${4:-300}"

    # Cache file per cycle
    local cache_file="${CACHE_DIR:-.}/.weekly_recommend_cache"

    # Get current cycle period
    local period_data=$(get_daily_period "$next_reset")
    local cycle_start=$(echo "$period_data" | jq -r '.start')

    # Check cache validity
    if [[ -f "$cache_file" ]]; then
        IFS='|' read -r cached_ts cached_cycle_start cached_value < "$cache_file"
        local current_ts=$(date +%s)

        # Cache valid if: same cycle AND within cache duration
        if [[ "$cached_cycle_start" == "$cycle_start" ]] && \
           [[ $((current_ts - cached_ts)) -lt $cache_duration ]] && \
           [[ "$cached_value" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
            echo "$cached_value"
            return 0
        fi
    fi

    # Get weekly cost at cycle start by filtering blocks
    local cycle_start_iso=$(timestamp_to_iso "$cycle_start")
    local weekly_period=$(get_anthropic_period "$next_reset")
    local period_start=$(echo "$weekly_period" | jq -r '.start')
    local period_start_iso=$(timestamp_to_iso "$period_start")

    # Get blocks and filter up to cycle start
    local blocks_data=$(cd ~ && npx --yes ccusage blocks --json --offline 2>/dev/null | awk '/^{/,0')

    if [[ -z "$blocks_data" || "$blocks_data" == "null" ]]; then
        echo "0"
        return
    fi

    # Calculate weekly cost at cycle start (from blocks before cycle started)
    local weekly_cost_at_cycle_start=$(echo "$blocks_data" | jq -r --arg start_iso "$period_start_iso" --arg end_iso "$cycle_start_iso" '
        [.blocks[] |
         select(
           (.startTime >= $start_iso) and
           (.startTime < $end_iso)
         ) |
         .costUSD
        ] | add // 0
    ')

    # Apply baseline offset (same as current week calculation)
    if [ "$(awk "BEGIN {print ($weekly_baseline_pct != 0)}")" = "1" ]; then
        local baseline_cost=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", ($weekly_limit * $weekly_baseline_pct) / 100}")
        weekly_cost_at_cycle_start=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", $weekly_cost_at_cycle_start + $baseline_cost}")
    fi

    # Calculate available budget at cycle start
    local avail_cost_at_start=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", $weekly_limit - $weekly_cost_at_cycle_start}")
    local avail_pct_at_start=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", ($avail_cost_at_start / $weekly_limit) * 100}")

    # Calculate cycles left from cycle start to reset
    local time_from_cycle_start_to_reset=$((next_reset - cycle_start))
    local cycles_left=$(awk "BEGIN {hours = $time_from_cycle_start_to_reset / 3600; cycles = hours / 24; print (cycles == int(cycles)) ? int(cycles) : int(cycles) + 1}")

    # Calculate recommend value
    local recommend_value=0
    if [ "$cycles_left" -gt 0 ]; then
        recommend_value=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.0f\", $avail_pct_at_start / $cycles_left}")
    fi

    # Cache the result
    local current_ts=$(date +%s)
    echo "${current_ts}|${cycle_start}|${recommend_value}" > "${cache_file}.tmp"
    mv "${cache_file}.tmp" "$cache_file"

    echo "$recommend_value"
}

# Calculate monthly period boundaries
# Returns current monthly period from payment_cycle_start_date to next month
# Usage: get_monthly_period <payment_cycle_start_timestamp>
# Returns: JSON with {start, end} timestamps
get_monthly_period() {
    local cycle_start="${1:?Missing payment_cycle_start timestamp}"

    # Get current time
    local current_time=$(date +%s)

    # Extract date components from cycle start
    local cycle_start_iso=$(timestamp_to_iso "$cycle_start")

    # Try GNU date first, then macOS date
    # Normalise ISO string for macOS date -j: Z → +0000, strip colon in +HH:MM
    local cycle_start_iso_norm=$(echo "$cycle_start_iso" | sed 's/Z$/+0000/; s/\([+-][0-9][0-9]\):\([0-9][0-9]\)$/\1\2/')

    local cycle_day=$(date -d "$cycle_start_iso" "+%d" 2>/dev/null) || true
    if [ -z "$cycle_day" ]; then
        cycle_day=$(date -j -f "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z" "$cycle_start_iso_norm" "+%d" 2>/dev/null) || true
    fi
    if [ -z "$cycle_day" ]; then
        cycle_day=$(date -j -u -f "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S" "${cycle_start_iso%%[Z+]*}" "+%d" 2>/dev/null)
    fi

    local cycle_time=$(date -d "$cycle_start_iso" "+%H:%M:%S" 2>/dev/null) || true
    if [ -z "$cycle_time" ]; then
        cycle_time=$(date -j -f "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z" "$cycle_start_iso_norm" "+%H:%M:%S" 2>/dev/null) || true
    fi
    if [ -z "$cycle_time" ]; then
        cycle_time=$(date -j -u -f "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S" "${cycle_start_iso%%[Z+]*}" "+%H:%M:%S" 2>/dev/null)
    fi

    # Get current year and month
    local current_year=$(date -d "@$current_time" "+%Y" 2>/dev/null) || true
    if [ -z "$current_year" ]; then
        current_year=$(date -j -r "$current_time" "+%Y")
    fi

    local current_month=$(date -d "@$current_time" "+%m" 2>/dev/null) || true
    if [ -z "$current_month" ]; then
        current_month=$(date -j -r "$current_time" "+%m")
    fi

    # Build current month's cycle start (same day and time, current month)
    local this_month_cycle=$(date -d "${current_year}-${current_month}-${cycle_day} ${cycle_time}" "+%s" 2>/dev/null) || true
    if [ -z "$this_month_cycle" ]; then
        this_month_cycle=$(date -j -f "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S" "${current_year}-${current_month}-${cycle_day} ${cycle_time}" "+%s" 2>/dev/null)
    fi

    # If current time is before this month's cycle start, use previous month
    if [ "$current_time" -lt "$this_month_cycle" ]; then
        # Go back one month
        if [ "$current_month" = "01" ]; then
            current_year=$((current_year - 1))
            current_month="12"
        else
            current_month=$(printf "%02d" $((10#$current_month - 1)))
        fi
        this_month_cycle=$(date -d "${current_year}-${current_month}-${cycle_day} ${cycle_time}" "+%s" 2>/dev/null) || true
        if [ -z "$this_month_cycle" ]; then
            this_month_cycle=$(date -j -f "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S" "${current_year}-${current_month}-${cycle_day} ${cycle_time}" "+%s" 2>/dev/null)
        fi
    fi

    # Calculate next month's cycle start
    local next_year="$current_year"
    local next_month=$(printf "%02d" $((10#$current_month + 1)))
    if [ "$next_month" = "13" ]; then
        next_year=$((current_year + 1))
        next_month="01"
    fi

    local next_month_cycle=$(date -d "${next_year}-${next_month}-${cycle_day} ${cycle_time}" "+%s" 2>/dev/null) || true
    if [ -z "$next_month_cycle" ]; then
        next_month_cycle=$(date -j -f "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S" "${next_year}-${next_month}-${cycle_day} ${cycle_time}" "+%s" 2>/dev/null)
    fi

    # Return period boundaries
    echo "{\"start\": $this_month_cycle, \"end\": $next_month_cycle}"
}

# Calculate monthly cost using ccusage blocks
# Filters blocks from payment cycle start to current time
# Usage: get_monthly_cost <payment_cycle_start_timestamp> [cache_duration_seconds]
# Returns: Total cost for current monthly period
get_monthly_cost() {
    local cycle_start="${1:?Missing payment_cycle_start timestamp}"
    local cache_duration="${2:-300}"  # Default 5 minutes

    # Get script directory
    local utils_dir="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
    local cache_file="$utils_dir/../data/.monthly_cache"
    local cache_duration_minutes=$((cache_duration / 60))

    # Get current monthly period boundaries
    local period_data=$(get_monthly_period "$cycle_start")
    local period_start=$(echo "$period_data" | jq -r '.start')
    local period_end=$(echo "$period_data" | jq -r '.end')

    # Check if cache exists and is fresh
    if [[ -f "$cache_file" ]]; then
        local cache_age=$(find "$cache_file" -mmin -${cache_duration_minutes} 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
        if [[ $cache_age -gt 0 ]]; then
            # Read cache and validate format: timestamp|period_start|period_end|monthly_cost
            local cache_content=$(cat "$cache_file")
            local cached_period_start=$(echo "$cache_content" | cut -d'|' -f2)
            local cached_cost=$(echo "$cache_content" | cut -d'|' -f4)

            # Validate: same period AND valid number
            if [[ "$cached_period_start" == "$period_start" ]] && [[ "$cached_cost" =~ ^[0-9]+\.?[0-9]*$ ]]; then
                echo "$cached_cost"
                return 0
            fi
        fi
    fi

    # Cache miss - calculate from ccusage
    # Convert timestamps to ISO format for ccusage
    local start_iso=$(timestamp_to_iso "$period_start")
    local end_iso=$(timestamp_to_iso "$period_end")

    # Get all blocks and filter by monthly period
    local blocks_data=$(cd ~ && npx --yes "ccusage@17.1.0" blocks --json --offline 2>/dev/null | awk '/^{/,0')

    # Filter blocks within period and sum costs
    local monthly_cost=$(echo "$blocks_data" | jq -r --arg start "$start_iso" --arg end "$end_iso" '
        [.blocks[]? |
         select(.startTime >= $start and .startTime < $end) |
         .costUSD // 0
        ] | add // 0
    ')

    # Format to 2 decimal places
    monthly_cost=$(printf "%.2f" "$monthly_cost")

    # Atomic cache write with period metadata
    local current_ts=$(date +%s)
    echo "${current_ts}|${period_start}|${period_end}|${monthly_cost}" > "${cache_file}.tmp"
    mv "${cache_file}.tmp" "$cache_file"

    echo "$monthly_cost"
}

# Calculate session cost using ccusage blocks
# Derives session start from the first entry in the transcript file, then sums
# block costs from that time onwards.
# Usage: get_session_cost <transcript_path>
# Returns: Total cost for the current session (float, 2 decimal places)
get_session_cost() {
    local transcript_path="${1:-}"

    # Derive session start from the transcript's first line timestamp
    local session_start_iso=""
    if [[ -n "$transcript_path" && -f "$transcript_path" ]]; then
        # Each transcript line is a JSON object; grab the first "timestamp" field
        session_start_iso=$(head -1 "$transcript_path" 2>/dev/null | \
            grep -o '"timestamp":"[^"]*"' | head -1 | \
            sed 's/"timestamp":"//; s/"//')
    fi

    if [[ -z "$session_start_iso" ]]; then
        # No transcript or no timestamp found — report 0 rather than crashing
        echo "0.00"
        return 0
    fi

    # Fetch all blocks (offline / cached pricing) and filter by session start
    local blocks_data
    blocks_data=$(cd ~ && npx --yes ccusage blocks --json --offline 2>/dev/null | awk '/^{/,0')

    if [[ -z "$blocks_data" || "$blocks_data" == "null" ]]; then
        echo "0.00"
        return 0
    fi

    # Sum costUSD for every block whose startTime >= session start
    local session_cost
    session_cost=$(echo "$blocks_data" | jq -r --arg start "$session_start_iso" '
        [.blocks[] |
         select(.startTime >= $start) |
         .costUSD
        ] | add // 0
    ')

    printf "%.2f" "$session_cost"
}

# Export functions for use in other scripts
if [[ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" != "${0}" ]]; then
    export -f timestamp_to_iso
    export -f iso_to_timestamp
    export -f get_anthropic_period
    export -f get_daily_period
    export -f get_monthly_period
    export -f get_daily_cost
    export -f get_official_weekly_cost
    export -f get_monthly_cost
    export -f get_weekly_recommend
    export -f get_session_cost
fi
